Bio: Seattle DJ and co-host of the Check the Volume weekly party at Q Nightclub. Known for indie dance and house music. Real name Holly Whitfield, a longtime fixture in the Seattle electronic music scene with Shameless.
Digital/analog DJ known for deep/minimal house, techno, breaks and acid. Part of Shameless crew since \~2013.

Levi Clark is a longtime Shameless resident whose musical background runs wider than a single lane, pulling from rock, electronica, hip-hop, classical, blues, jazz, breaks, and techno. Early event listings already had him sharing stages with national headliners while sharpening a taste for dirty, body-moving electronic music. These days, his presence on Shameless lineups reads less like a booking note and more like infrastructure: someone who understands the room, the crew, and the culture from the inside. Levi brings the kind of resident energy every real party needs,flexible, grounded, and fully committed to making people dance.

Jen Woolfe is a Shameless resident with a long arc through Seattle, San Francisco, and back again, carrying deep house and techno with the perspective of someone who has lived in multiple West Coast dance ecosystems. Her sets lean into groove, mood, and the kind of resident-level patience that makes a party feel held instead of merely programmed. She understands Shameless from the inside, which means the selections can serve the room without flattening her personality. Jen brings a trusted hand to the booth: deep, warm, and quietly built for the long haul.

Wesley Holmes is a Seattle house-music lifer whose influence in the Pacific Northwest stretches across more than 25 years of parties, records, rooms, and dancers. A DJ's DJ with deep local credibility, he has helped keep the city's house lineage connected to soul, groove, and proper late-night function. His sets carry the ease of someone who does not need to force authority; the records do the talking, and the room follows. Wesley brings foundational Seattle heat,seasoned, generous, and still fully alive on the floor.

Yo Yo Yoga is Annie Evasick's Seattle-born yoga and live-DJ project, founded in 2010 at Electric Tea Garden while she was working with Shameless Productions and Decibel Festival. The project connects underground house and techno culture with movement, recovery, and community, bringing DJ-led yoga events to clubs, rooftops, festivals, streams, and wellness spaces without losing its dance-floor roots.

Tyson Wittrock is a longtime Seattle DJ connected to Shameless event history, Breakfast Club, Decibel-week afterhours, Cascadia Northwest, and collaborative bills with Recess. His lineup history places him alongside Christian Martin, Pezzner, Codebase, Joe Bellingham, Adlib, Innerflight, Bottom Forty, and other Northwest underground players. His profile sits in the older house and techno backbone of the crew's 2010s era: steady, local, trusted, and built around real scene presence.

Joe Bellingham is a Seattle DJ, producer, and live electronic act with a long Shameless history, joining the collective in 2009 and performing at Electric Tea Garden and other local rooms. His live-facing work points to a producer and performer rooted in Seattle underground electronic music rather than a straight DJ-only profile.

Jon Lee is a Seattle DJ with roots stretching back to 1994 and a long-running commitment to deep, minimal, and underground house. As the founder of Concepts Records, he has been part of the city's connective tissue between local selectors, producers, and dancers who prefer substance over flash. His releases and mixes keep a steady focus on groove, restraint, and late-night utility,the kind of sound that rewards people who stay on the floor past the obvious moment. Jon Lee brings veteran pressure with a clean hand: patient, deep, and built for rooms that know how to listen while they move.

Ramiro is Ramiro Gutierrez, founder of Uniting Souls and one of Seattle house music's true community architects. Since founding Uniting Souls in 1997 and making Seattle home in 2000, he has produced and performed at hundreds of events, helped build the Uniting Souls Music label, and earned descriptions like "Seattle house music institution" and "West Coast legend." His DJ sets are built around connection, storytelling, groove, and the belief that house music thrives when the room feels welcome. Ramiro brings the full package: history, heart, label-head discipline, and dance-floor generosity.
Seattle DJ and Shameless crew member, known for queer dance events and Latinx community-focused nights.

Saqib is a Brooklyn-based DJ, producer, and label head whose records move with the loose, hypnotic confidence of a room that knows it is in good hands. Founder of Beats On Time, he has built a catalog around forward-leaning house, indie dance, and left-field club music, with releases and affiliations stretching through Kindisch, Desert Hearts, Abracadabra, and Acid Tourism. His sets carry that same signature: elastic percussion, warm psychedelia, and enough narrative lift to keep dancers locked in without sanding down the weird edges. Saqib lands as a true party architect,musical, mischievous, and tuned to the communal side of the dance floor.

Brian Lyons is one of Seattle house music's steady hands, a Flammable founder and longtime champion of underground dance music. His sets carry the patience of someone who knows how to build a room instead of just hit it with peaks. Around Shameless, that makes him exactly the kind of selector who can turn a rooftop, basement, or late-night room into a proper Seattle house gathering. Deep grooves, warm pressure, and no wasted motion.

Copeland (Erika) discovered Seattle's underground electronic music scene in 2008, becoming a devoted patron of the dance floor before transitioning to DJing in 2021. A Vitala Recordings resident and promoter, she has performed at festivals including Sun Worship, Afterglow, Cascadia, and Panacea, as well as events for the Dusty Booty Ranch and Fireburn, Flammable, Uniting Souls, and Studio 4/4. Known for her deep connection to the music community, she intuitively crafts sets spanning techno and deep house across Seattle venues like Monkey Loft, Madame Lou's, and Cherry.

Riz Rollins is a Seattle institution: DJ Riz, longtime KEXP voice, Expansions host, and a defining presence in the city's gay, Black, hip-hop, and electronic dance communities. His history runs from Chicago roots to decades of Seattle clubs, radio booths, basements, and dance floors, with sets that can move through soul, gospel, funk, hip-hop, house, drum & bass, and left-field magic without breaking the spell. Riz does not just play records; he tells people who they are allowed to become for a few hours. He brings blessing and mischief in equal measure: reverend, selector, troublemaker, and living archive.

Portland-based DJ, musical artist, event producer, and co-owner of Koritsi Komma Records. DJing since 1999, starting in Bellingham, WA before relocating to Portland. Known for eclectic, LGBTQ+-inclusive sets and advocacy. Resident at Bottom Forty (Seattle) and Underground SF; also plays CC Slaughter's and The Eagle in Portland.

GriffinGrrl is a Seattle DJ and dance-music catalyst with roots in the 1990s Los Angeles rave scene. That history gives her sets a lived-in understanding of rave culture as more than aesthetics: rhythm, release, community, and a willingness to chase the ecstatic edge. Her C89.5 guest-mix presence points to the same thing her local reputation does,an energetic selector who knows how to bring momentum without losing personality. GriffinGrrl brings veteran spark with West Coast muscle and a direct line to why people fell in love with this music in the first place.

HAÜS\\GASM is a queer Seattle DJ project built for house, tech, techno, indie dance, and disco pressure. The name says party, but the lane is sharper than novelty: sweaty, playful club music with enough range to work a Breakfast Club dance floor and enough attitude to keep it from going beige. Their sets move through queer nightlife's best instincts,release, flirtation, rhythm, and a little chaos in the corners. HAÜS\\GASM brings exactly the right temperature: hot, unruly, and aimed at bodies first.

Jon Lemmon is a Seattle DJ, producer, radio host, and founder of Viva Recordings, one of the city's long-running deep-house institutions. His work around Viva connects label culture, DJ craft, and community programming, keeping Seattle's house pulse steady across clubs, radio, and released music. Behind the decks, Lemmon favors deep, warm, functional grooves that build trust before they build drama. He brings label-head discipline with Northwest looseness: soulful pressure, clean momentum, and a room-first sense of what house music is for.
Seattle DJ and KEXP radio host presenting reggae on Positive Vibrations and electronic music on Expansions. Releases on Hospital Records, Ninja Tune, Get Physical.

Luke Mandala is a San Diego-based live electronic performer who bridges acoustic musicianship and machine-driven dance music. His setup folds in live wind instruments, drumming, and electronic production, giving the performance a human pulse that can stretch beyond standard DJ architecture. The result lands somewhere between ritual, jam, and club system: organic textures wrapped around dance-floor motion. Luke Mandala brings livewire festival energy with actual musicianship behind it,earthy, psychedelic, and built to move.

Michael Manahan has been a mover in Pacific Northwest underground arts and music culture for more than two decades. Based between the Cascade foothills, Seattle, and Portland, he works as a DJ, event producer, music producer, and creative force behind Hunt & Gather Recordings and Cascadia NW Arts and Music Festival. His sets sit comfortably between ambient depth, bass weight, and deep club focus, which makes him more than just another name on a lineup. Manahan brings regional history, outdoor-festival spirit, and the kind of patient pressure that knows how to stretch a night open.

Nark is Kevin Kauer, a Seattle/Palm Springs DJ, producer, photographer, designer, and nightlife builder behind Nark Magazine, Bottom Forty, and Massive. His work helped push Seattle queer club culture toward something stranger, sexier, and more open, with parties that made room for disco, acid house, techno, drag, fashion, and late-night weirdness. Behind the decks, Nark brings analog warmth, queer-club pressure, and the confidence of someone who helped build the room he is playing in.

Sharlese is Sharlese Metcalf, a Seattle DJ, KEXP host, producer, and tireless local-music connector with roots in radio going back to 2001. Her path runs through KGRG, KEXP's Audioasis, the goth/industrial/wave crew Second Sight, Audiodrome at Pony, Mechanical Breakdown, Expansions, and countless local dance floors. As a selector, she brings hypnotic transitions, techno, synth, wave, and underground electronic music with a sharp ear for mood and history. Sharlese brings the real Seattle frequency: informed, dark-edged, community-deep, and never generic.

Snapdragon (real name Denise Zhang) is a Seattle-based DJ and electronic producer, also known by the alias Yung Futon. A member of the CUSTOMS collective, she has gained recognition through KEXP's Midnight in a Perfect World for her propulsive progressive tech-house sets. Her music takes listeners on energetic journeys through electronic soundscapes, earning her a growing following in the Pacific Northwest underground scene.

Seattle DJ and producer known as Thorson (aka Krisco). Real name Kris Thorson Kellogg. Rocking dance floors in the Pacific Northwest since 2012, specializing in Disco, Funk, and House music. Has performed at Shameless Retreat and various PNW venues.

WD4D is Waylon Dungan, a Seattle DJ and producer whose sets braid futuristic hip-hop, R\&B, soul, global rhythm, and electronic music into something generous and deeply physical. Dubbed a feel-good force in local DJ culture, he has spent years proving that technical skill and emotional warmth do not need to be opposites. His selections can get heady, but the center is always human: drums, swing, melody, and a room that feels invited rather than instructed. WD4D brings open-hearted range,beat-scene intelligence with block-party gravity.

Adlib is Devin Kelly, the Seattle DJ, producer, and promoter whose work with Shameless and People of Earth has helped keep the city's underground mischievous and moving. A self-taught DJ since 1998, he came up through Idaho, Boise, and Salt Lake City scenes before becoming a regular force across Western clubs, renegades, and outdoor gatherings. His sets are known for technical blends, darker progressive pressure, acid, techno, and enough crowd-rocking instinct to keep the heady stuff from floating away. Adlib is pure Shameless DNA: radical good times, serious tunes, and dance-floor debauchery with a working brain.
Seattle-based DJ/producer specializing in house and adjacent dance music. Plays at Monkey Loft, LOST, Uniting Souls, Studio 4/4.

Black Velveteen (real name Chris Offutt) is a pillar of Seattle's dance music scene, originally from the South Side of Chicago. Navigating the underground scenes from Chicago to Seattle to San Francisco and back again, Offutt crafts dark, deep, and atmospheric electronic music with active releases on SoundCloud and an integrated show on Proton Radio. Their sound blends deep, ethereal, percussive, melodic, and sometimes dark elements, earning recognition as a creator, artist, writer, and DJ/producer in the Pacific Northwest.
Seattle-based DJ and producer, born in Capitol Hill, crafting bass-forward, dark melodic, atmospheric soundscapes with world music textures.

Doza is a Seattle open-format DJ who debuted under the name in 2017 and built his lane through community gatherings, club sets, and high-energy local rooms. His strength is range: reading the floor, moving quickly, and making genre shifts feel like part of the same conversation instead of a playlist panic. That kind of flexibility matters in a city where parties pull from house, hip-hop, bass, pop, and underground dance culture at once. Doza brings utility with personality,fast hands, crowd sense, and no fear of turning the room up.

Miss Min.D is Mindy Christman, a Seattle multi-genre DJ, producer, promoter, and community builder whose roots run from Wyoming into the city's underground dance scene. Active in Seattle since 2004 and DJing since the vinyl era, she helped found Skunky Beats, DJ4NORML, and Fraktured, then became one of the city's most trusted names for breaks, house, bass, and drum & bass pressure. Public bios and interviews point to long-running residencies and performances across Last Supper Club, Club Contour, Noc Noc, The High Dive, Still Liquor, Kremwerk, and C89.5. Miss Min.D brings a rare mix of floor craft and community muscle: high-energy selections, emotional range, and a resident-level ability to make a room move without flattening her personality.

Nayiram is a Ghanaian-born, Seattle-based DJ pushing Afrobeats, Amapiano, Afro House, dancehall, and global club sounds through the city's nightlife. His work around The Afrobeats Party and Global Bounce has helped give Seattle a consistent, community-centered space for African music and Black dance-floor culture. On the decks, he moves like a connector, folding popular anthems, underground edits, and diasporic rhythms into one room. That energy opens the floor wider and makes the party feel bigger than one scene.

Porky is David Leon, the San Diego native and Desert Hearts wildcard whose lighthearted name hides a very serious commitment to the party. Raised around the same Southern California underground orbit as the Desert Hearts crew, he built his reputation through colorful, high-energy house and techno sets at Desert Hearts weekenders, Burning Man, Tulum, and City Hearts events across North America. His style is playful without being disposable: weird techno corners, house crowd-pleasers, big personality, and a selector's instinct for turning records into moments.

Soon(ish) is Jonathan Cats, a Seattle DJ, Noise Complaint co-founder, and constant flag-bearer for the city's house and techno scene. His SoundCloud bio puts the mission plainly: disco, rolling tech house, deeper 4/4 sounds, and one goal, to make people dance. He has represented Seattle at EDC, Upstream, Cascadia, and Holy Ship, while Noise Complaint has thrown parties from Seattle to San Francisco, Denver, San Diego, New York, and beyond. In Shameless language, Soon(ish) is reliable pressure with hometown pride: the kind of set that knows the room, works the room, and leaves it sweaty.
Seattle DJ, alias of Jess Duran. Driving force in Seattle's electronic music scene since 2013. Played at Nowadays NYC, The Lot Radio.

Tait Modern is a Seattle DJ, promoter, and community builder woven into the city's underground dance fabric. His SoundCloud bio traces a path through Uniting Souls, Electric Tea Garden, Cascadia, NightJar, punk roots, drum & bass roots, and a current house sound with hard edges. He brings tough, generous house selections with local memory in the grooves.

Seattle-based DJ and producer (real name Tom Butcher). Codebase is his primary solo electronic project, focused on dance-oriented tracks. Active in the Shameless and PNW underground community; also performed at the Deck'd Out rooftop series at Monkey Loft.
Los Angeles-based DJ and co-owner of the Superfreq brand with Mr.C. Known for dark and underground electronic music with decades of experience.
Los Angeles-based DJ and Desert Hearts resident. Known for dark, sinister, and deep electronic music, playing the latest hours at clubs and festivals.

J.Phlip is Jessica Phillippe, a San Francisco-based DJ and producer who became one of Dirtybird's founding originals and helped carry its bass-heavy, playful house sound around the world. Her résumé includes serious rooms and festivals,Movement Detroit, Coachella, Fabric London, Space Ibiza,but the real point is her instinct for weird, tough, body-jacking records. She can go funky, dark, stripped, or rude without losing the floor's trust. J.Phlip brings West Coast club mischief at expert level: dirty low end, sharp turns, and a selector's refusal to behave.
San Francisco native now residing in the Seattle area. DJ with 20+ years of dance floor experience across NYC, Houston, SF, and Seattle. Known for cross-genre sets.
Seattle DJ and Uniting Souls Music resident. Her exuberance for art is directly tied to her passion for music, playing deep house and electronic music.

Lucky Cheese is a Seattle DJ with roots in Chile and Washington. Her sets move through eclectic club sounds with a warm, playful touch, bringing local underground energy without getting locked into one lane.
Finnish DJ and producer centered around deep, cerebral, and introspective house and techno. Dark, ethereal visuals reminiscent of her Finnish heritage. Regular at Seattle's Monkey Loft.

Shelrawka, also billed as Miss Shelrawka, is a Seattle DJ who made her mark locally after relocating from New York City in 2008. Recent Kremwerk listings describe her as a highly sought-after selector with more than 20 years of experience, precise mixing, and a multi-genre approach shaped by techno, minimal, and underground club music.
Seattle-based DJ duo known for their sets at Deck'd Out and Shameless events. Part of the Seattle underground electronic music community.
Seattle DJ who began DJing in 2019 and is a member of the Tremulant collective. Known for cosmic and cerebral electronic music.
Seattle DJ, founder of Upper Left Events and Studio 44. Part of the LOST DJ crew and Monkey Loft resident. Known for bringing top-tier underground bookings to Seattle.
German electronic music duo (Holger and Kai) based in Berlin. Releases on Get Physical, Culprit, My Favourite Robot, and Mobilee. International touring act with a signature tech house sound.
Seattle DJ raised on acid house and disco between Detroit and Chicago. Found his voice DJing at radical faerie gatherings, Burning Man, and underground clubs in the Pacific Northwest.

DJ Trinitron is a Seattle-based DJ with a lifelong pull toward electronic music, tracing the obsession back to the early 1990s. As a Viva Recordings resident and the creator of Bounce Brunch, he sits comfortably in the city's house ecosystem while keeping a party-forward sense of fun. His approach is rooted in enthusiasm rather than posturing: bright selections, community energy, and enough range to make daytime joy and late-night movement feel connected. Trinitron brings clean local lift,house music with a smile, a pulse, and real Seattle context.
Seattle DJ Mike Zaretzke, known as Zeebo. A true soldier for the underground community both on the dance floor and behind the decks. Part of Dusty Booty Ranch, Flammable, LOST, and Studio 4/4 crews.
Seattle DJ and member of the Board of Deacons alongside Riz Rollins, Devlin Jenkins, and Joey Webb. Active in Seattle's underground house music community.

Seattle DJ, producer, and electronic music figure for over two decades. Real name Brit Hansen. Co-founded the beloved duo Jacob London with Dave Pezzner, releasing funky underground tech-house 12-inches throughout the 2000s. Also active as a solo artist as Hanssen. Guest DJ for KEXP's Midnight in a Perfect World. Featured at Deck'd Out and Shameless events.

Chris Tower is a Seattle-based DJ, producer, and Opulent Temple regular with a sound built around progressive house, tech house, melodic techno, and sun-baked festival lift. Since starting behind the decks in 2009, he has become a steady presence in Seattle's underground and Burning Man-connected communities, including years producing Seattle Opulent Temple fundraisers. His productions, mixes, and playa-informed sets aim for scale without losing warmth: big melodies, rolling low end, and enough emotional pull to keep the room open. Chris Tower brings altitude,high-energy house music with community roots and a clear sense of occasion.

Claudia LB is a Seattle-based queer Indigenous DJ and the force behind Fricktion Collective and Club Milk. Her sound pushes hypnotic, groove-driven techno, trance, club, and bouncy Eurodance, blending dark rolling low end with trance-washed melody and forward-moving rhythm. That combination gives her sets a specific charge: emotional, locked-in, and physical without defaulting to obvious peak-time formulas. Claudia LB brings new-generation Seattle underground energy with purpose, pressure, and a clear point of view.

Dane Garfield is a Seattle house DJ tied into the Flammable family, the long-running Sunday institution that has treated house music like church for more than three decades. Recent lineups place him at Chop Suey, Monkey Loft, Gas Works Park, and Shameless/Flammable collaborations, which says plenty about where he sits in the local ecosystem. His lane is not about celebrity gloss; it is about showing up for the room, the weekly, and the dancers who keep Seattle house culture alive. Dane brings working-DJ value: reliable heat, local continuity, and no need to over-explain the assignment.
Seattle DJ who has graced the Seattle music scene with talent and knowledge since the early 1990s. Active in Flammable and the Seattle underground scene.
Seattle DJ known for house music. Active in Seattle nightlife alongside Miss Min D and other Seattle DJs.
House DJ based in Seattle, WA. Born in CA, raised in NC. Made her Shameless debut in 2021. Known for bringing energetic, community-centered sets to Seattle's underground.
Long-time participant in the San Francisco underground scene, now residing in Seattle. Plays deep, progressive, tech, and melodic house and techno.
Seattle-based DJ, producer, and label founder. Active in the Seattle music community, bringing fire to the dance floor at venues throughout the city.
Seattle DJ Karen Baskett with deep, soulful, disco-influenced sound. Moved from NYC to Seattle in 2004. Flammable Sundays resident DJ at Chop Suey.

Jared Love is a Vancouver, BC-based DJ and producer whose music builds on influences ranging from jazz and R\&B to early electronica. Emerging in the early 2010s in Vancouver's burgeoning underground, he earned residencies at staples like Gorg-O-Mish Afterhours and Soma Music Hub. His releases have charted internationally, including collaborations with Jay Tripwire, and he has performed at notable events including opening for Traumer. His hypnotic, groove-driven style is rooted in Vancouver's underground dance floor culture.
Seattle DJ featured in Shameless Audiocast. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music community.
World-touring DJ and co-owner of Plump Records. Known for releases on Hot Creations, Crosstown Rebels, Dirtybird, and more. Regular performer at major festivals.

Kyle Douglas is a Chicago-born, Seattle-based DJ and selector with a deep-house center and a mentor's relationship to the local scene. His House Cleaning Records link points to the practical work of keeping house music circulating: sharing records, shaping taste, and helping the next wave understand the culture behind the groove. His sets lean into warmth, swing, and patience rather than cheap impact. Kyle brings caretaker energy with dance-floor purpose,deep crates, clean intent, and a steady hand on the room.

Nazo is a Seattle-based house DJ originally from Soweto, South Africa, bringing Afro House and African-influenced electronic music into the local underground. His sound sits where rhythm, heritage, and club function overlap, favoring percussion, warmth, and a grounded sense of movement over empty spectacle. In a city often pulled toward European and American dance lineages, Nazo adds a needed rhythmic vocabulary and a different emotional center. He brings deep diasporic groove: sun, sweat, and a floor that moves from the hips outward.

Peter Evans is a Seattle-based Viva Recordings DJ with a steady archive of local sets, radio mixes, and club recordings. His public SoundCloud history includes Viva Recordings dates, C89.5 The Vortex, Proton Featured Artist, Diffusion, and multiple Monkey Loft appearances. His sound sits in the deep-house lane: polished, patient, and built around groove instead of spectacle.
Seattle-based vocalist, songwriter, guitarist, pianist, and DJ. Born in Cleveland, OH and now thriving in Seattle. Known for both live performances and DJ sets.

Rex is a Seattle DJ with an open-format style built around turntable skill, quick transitions, and crowd-focused wordplay. His sets pull from soulful rhythms, club energy, and dance-floor instincts, giving him a flexible lane in Seattle's underground party circuit.
Seattle DJ duo featuring Riz Rollins and Rob Green. Riz is a 35+ year KEXP radio personality, venerated Seattle fixture at gay, hip-hop and electronic dance parties.

Rob Noble is a Seattle drum & bass and bass-music DJ with deep ties to the DEEP N BASS world and the wider PNW underground. Event bios describe him as a dedicated performer with more than a decade behind the decks, known primarily for drum & bass while refusing to let genre labels fence in his taste. That breadth makes his sets hit differently: melodic, heavy, fluid, and willing to move between speed, pressure, and atmosphere. Rob Noble brings low-end discipline from a scene that knows endurance, precision, and how to make a system work.

Sassmouth is a Chicago-based DJ with Seattle ties whose sets move playfully through techno, house, acid, electro, and whatever else can make a room snap awake. A smartbar-level selector with a high-energy, eclectic reputation, she brings Midwest club knowledge without making it feel academic. Her style is fun in the dangerous sense: quick turns, weird records, and a refusal to let the night settle into autopilot. Sassmouth brings exactly the right kind of trouble,sharp, queer-adjacent club energy with a grin and a kick drum.
Seattle DJ triple threat Shomari Shanks. In the Seattle music scene since 1989 at age 15. Known for soulful house music sets.
Seattle-based DJ and event producer born and raised on Capitol Hill. Known for dark, moody deep house and techno. Studio 4/4 resident. Plays Flammable, Shameless, and Viva events.
Seattle-based DJ and bass music producer in the PNW electronic music community. Known for high-energy sets blending bass music genres. Active on SoundCloud and in the Seattle underground scene.
San Francisco-based DJ and producer, prime player in the Bay Area underground dance music scene for decades. Co-founder of Squirrels On Film label.
Seattle DJ, record collector, and KEXP On Air personality. Internationally recognized hip-hop DJ, founder of Conception Records, and member of The Sharpshooters.

Tara Brooks is a Southern California DJ and producer whose deep, hypnotic sound has made her a natural fit for Desert Hearts and West Coast underground circles. Since 2007, she has built a reputation around eclectic, emotionally tuned sets that move through deep grooves, melodic tension, and psychedelic late-night pressure. Her music is polished but not sterile, spiritual but not soft, and always aimed at the long arc of a dance floor. Tara brings desert-tested momentum: sensual, driving, and wide open under the stars.
Seattle vinyl DJ, promoter, and event curator (Isabela Garcia). Founder of Babe Night and co-founder of Danse Macabre. Active in the Seattle underground scene for nearly a decade.

Seattle-based DJ and electronic music collective active in the PNW underground scene. Runs the ASW Mix Series, featuring underground electronic artists from around the world. Active in the Shameless community and related Seattle events.

Seattle-based certified yoga instructor and community figure, a regular at Shameless Productions events. Featured at Reverie Society daytime parties at Monkey Loft, co-presented by Shameless, Uniting Souls, and Viva Recordings. Leads live yoga sessions alongside electronic music DJs.

Andy Warren is a Portland-based DJ and co-founder of Believe You Me and Process PDX, two names tied to the Pacific Northwest's deeper underground dance network. His work as a promoter and selector has helped build rooms where house, techno, and left-of-center club music can breathe outside the obvious circuit. As a DJ, he brings the patience of someone who understands both sides of the booth: the architecture of the night and the records that make it worth staying. Andy brings Portland's understated depth,smart pacing, durable grooves, and no unnecessary gloss.
Seattle-via-SoCal DJ and event promoter, founder of Routine,a monthly event series at Ballard's Substation venue. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music scene.
Seattle DJ Jyoti B.Fly, 19-year DJ veteran and KEXP radio host of Wo'Pop world music show. Caravan Seattle resident known for global house, breaks, and funk.

Bizio is Nico Bisio, a Portland-based DJ and promoter active in Oregon's underground electronic community. His Healing House Music identity points toward a broad, psychedelic lane where house, techno, and communal dance culture meet without getting precious. As both organizer and selector, he understands the practical work behind a good room: sound, flow, trust, and the small decisions that let people open up. Bizio brings Northwest ritual energy,earthy, heady, and still fully committed to the kick drum.
Seattle renegade brass band dedicated to keeping the tradition of music, performance, energy, and sheer fun alive in the streets. Known for high-decibel immersive street shows.
Seattle DJ with South African roots. Debuted in Seattle in 2018, blending afro house, deep house, and acid jazz. Has played Shameless Deck'd Out at Monkey Loft.
San Francisco-based DJ and founder of Trippy Ass Technologies. Known for house, techno, and DnB. Has released music on Dirtybird, Get Physical, Crosstown Rebels, and more.
Berlin-based musician and DJ Luca Venezia, blending acid, post-punk, and dark disco. Latest album Another Heaven on Italians Do It Better. Performed at Shameless 23 Year Anniversary.
Miami-based DJ Christopher Milo, underground electronic music stalwart since the early 1990s. Has held residencies at NYC's Twilo, Cielo, Output, and more, plus global festival appearances.

DJ Weak Acid, Nick Bigelow, is a Seattle DJ and electronic artist known for live modular techno. A Patchwerks-connected performer, Weak Acid has played Basement State shows alongside artists like Project 32 and Magic 3ye, and has appeared with the Patchwerks family at Monkey Loft. The sound is hands-on, analog, and built from machines in real time.
Seattle-based DJ, active in the Seattle underground electronic music scene. Played Reverie Society events at Monkey Loft alongside DJ Weak Acid and other Seattle DJs.
Seattle-based DJ and producer born in Tarzana, CA. Released music on Dirtybird, Get Physical, Crosstown Rebels, Visionquest, and Strictly Rhythm. In-house composer at Can Can Cabaret.
Seattle-based musician and DJ. Part of Deep Light Recordings, Uniting Souls Music, and the Elevated Frequencies collective. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music community.

Dirty Vizualz is a Seattle live VJ and visuals project tied to Shameless' Deck'd Out programming at Monkey Loft. Event listings place Dirty Vizualz on Pride and summer rooftop bills with Open House Collective, IDLE, Hercules & Love Affair, DJHoodriah, Jen Woolfe, HAUS\
Instagram: GASM, Alfonso Tan, and Your MOM. The project adds motion, color, and live image work to the party environment, helping the rooftop and loft stages feel more immersive without pulling focus from the music.

Bio: Emily Song is a seasoned Seattle DJ whose path started in 1997 with vinyl sets at underground warehouse parties across the Pacific Northwest. Now connected to Viva Recordings, she carries the history of Seattle house culture with the ease of someone who has lived through more than one version of the scene. Her sound is rooted in house music's social function: steady rhythm, warm selections, and the belief that a dance floor is built through trust rather than tricks. Emily Song brings elder-floor energy,deeply local, quietly powerful, and fully fluent in the language of late nights.

Fern is a Seattle-based DJ in the PNW electronic music community, active in underground dance events across the Pacific Northwest. A dedicated contributor to Seattle's underground scene, Fern brings soulful and underground electronic selections to dance floors, supporting the local community through performances at Shameless events and local venues.
Frida K is a Seattle-based house DJ and producer originally from Mexico. Her sound leans groovy, bright, and direct, with live sets and releases built for dancers who want motion without over-polish.
Seattle musical collective described as "super group of musical magical weirdoes." Eclectic performers known for mixing styles and pushing the boundaries of fun.
New York-born internationally touring DJ, producer, radio host, yoga teacher, and label boss. Voted Best NYC DJ by Village Voice. Releases on Ultra Music, Ministry of Sound, Defected, and more.

Ginkgo is Robert Ginkgo, a Portland-linked DJ and co-curator of Believe You Me PDX and Your Sunday Best. His RA history spans Portland and Seattle, while his SoundCloud points to residency work with Believe You Me, Silicon Radio, and Process PDX. His lane is broader Pacific Northwest house culture with a Portland center of gravity: warm, underground, crate-conscious, and built for rooms that like subtle pressure.
Seattle electronic music producer and DJ (also known as Jacob London). Co-host of Expansions on KEXP radio, blending electronic and dance genres.
Seattle DJ and music producer Ismael Lukata, with Palestinian heritage and Spanish background. Making waves in Seattle's scene with bold, danceable sounds.

DJ Joy is Joy Sgobba, a Seattle-based DJ originally from San Diego with an open-format, multi-genre approach built around pure dance-floor service. Her own profile names house, bass, breaks, disco, electronica, indie, hip-hop, old-school, funk, downtempo, Top 40, and more, which makes her less a genre specialist than a party translator. The through-line is simple: she loves music, reads rooms, and wants people moving whether the setting is a club, private party, festival, or community event. DJ Joy brings exactly what the name promises,range, momentum, and no shame in rocking the floor.

James Flavour is Felix Eder, the German DJ and producer best known as one half of Dirt Crew alongside Break 3000. Since the mid-2000s, Dirt Crew's mix of old-school house and new-school techno has earned international dance-floor traction, with releases and remixes tied to labels such as MBF, Moodmusic, Highgrade, Trapez, and their own Dirt Crew Recordings. Flavour's sound carries that label-head sensibility: groovy, sturdy, and built from records that know their way around a sweaty room. James Flavour brings classic European house-techno muscle with enough funk to keep the edges loose.

Jamie Schwabl is a Seattle DJ and producer connected to the city's underground through Shameless, Tremulant, and related local circles. Her releases and sets sit in the functional zone where house, techno, and after-hours mood can overlap without announcing every transition. She brings the kind of scene-level presence that matters more than hype: showing up, developing sound, and helping keep the local current moving. Jamie brings focused hometown pressure,clean, grounded, and built for dancers who stay late.
Argentinian house and techno DJ and producer (Juan Ogaz). Has played alongside Tim Green, Lee Burridge, and others. Active in the Seattle and NYC underground scenes.

Jason Woo is a Seattle DJ connected to the Studio 4/4 and Monkey Loft circuit, with listings placing him on support bills for Lost Desert, Eli Escobar, Sama' Abdulhadi, Opulent Temple's Sacred Dance, and Shameless' Memorial Day Hijinks. He has appeared alongside Seattle names including Copeland, Ramiro, Zeebo, Brian Lyons, Sean Majors, Fouad Masoud, and Simone BG. His sets bring a polished house and techno sensibility with dependable local support-slot energy.
Seattle native DJ and producer. On Uniting Souls Music, Hood Politics Records, OFDM Records, and Feral One Records. Known for eclectic house, techno, and breaks.
Seattle DJ inspired by KCMU's DJ Riz. Active in the Shameless and Seattle underground electronic music community.

KINDA is a Seattle DJ tied to the Studio 4/4 resident crew, with public mixes on C89.5 and Peculiar Beats. Their SoundCloud also identifies KINDA as one half of Ravennas and one half of Pinky Promise, giving the project a clear local context. The sound points toward Seattle club culture with a taste for house and electronic sets that can work both radio and room.
Macedonia-native and longtime Seattle resident. DJ, producer, promoter, and record label head active in the Pacific Northwest music community.
Seattle DJ luminary and longtime fixture in the PNW electronic music scene. Active in Innerflight Music and related Seattle underground electronic events.

Lauren Ritter is a New York-based DJ, producer, and founder of Rift Vision, known for house music that favors groove, patience, and emotional detail over easy peak-time tricks. A longtime presence around the Resolute orbit, she brings the kind of selector discipline that turns a room slowly, letting rhythm and atmosphere do the heavy lifting. Her productions and DJ sets sit in that sweet spot between deep, trippy, and functional,music for dancers who like their hypnosis with a little bite. Lauren is the after-hours compass: elegant pressure, strange corners, and a pulse that keeps unfolding.

Leah York is a Seattle DJ and producer working across Latin club, techno, groove, reggaeton, perreo, dembow, future house, and Latin house energy. Her public links point to original releases like "Menealo," video sets, and a lane built around movement-first, bilingual, global dance sounds. She is not just playing generic club records; she is pulling Seattle into a sharper Latin electronic conversation. Leah brings color, bounce, and the kind of rhythm that opens up the floor fast.

Lola K is a Seattle-based DJ and producer pushing bright, club-ready house with enough grit to keep it underground. Her releases and credits connect her to Late Night Munchies, Brainjack, and Paradise Music, placing her in a lane where local hustle meets proper dance-floor utility. Behind the decks she keeps things agile, moving through house, tech-house, and late-night rollers without losing the party thread. Lola K brings hometown voltage: polished enough to travel, raw enough to keep the room sweating.

Lubelski is a Los Angeles-based producer and DJ whose hardware-heavy approach turns house music into something loose, psychedelic, and joyfully unpredictable. His catalog spans Dirtybird, Desert Hearts, Crosstown Rebels, Gruuv, Club Bad, and MOAN, with the 2022 album Happy Accidents showing the full range of his studio instincts. As a DJ, he has carried that weird-funk sensibility into major clubs and festivals including Lightning in a Bottle, Ultra Miami, Holy Ship\! Wrecked, Splash House, Elsewhere, Space Miami, and Public Works. Lubelski is a natural fit: playful machine music with serious low-end craft and no interest in coloring inside the lines.
Seattle DJ and enthusiastic selector with a crate spanning 90s rave to current sounds. Regular at Monkey Loft.

Meow Pals is a Seattle/Tremulant-linked DJ project documented on Kremwerk and DICE listings from the city's deeper club ecosystem. The name appears on Tremulant bills alongside Niki Sadeki, Zeebo, Parham, Captain Tremulant, Monotropa, and Jon-Paul Petry, placing the project in a psychedelic, melodic, small-room lane. The feel is niche and local in the best way: real scene presence, weird corners, and enough mystery to keep the room curious.

Milk & Honey is the Seattle duo of Everett Leftside and Zeebo, documented around Kremwerk's Tremulant orbit and centered on warm, drum-forward house music. Everett Leftside is also known as a MIND at LARGE resident, with sets moving through afro, melodic, deep, and tech tracks built around rhythm and introspection. Zeebo anchors the other half of the pairing, tying the project back to Seattle's deeper house community.

Monotropa is a Seattle-area DJ connected to the deeper, feel-forward side of the local underground. RA listings place them with Diffusion and CARAVAN lineups, moving through deep house, global sounds, and intimate basement energy. The existing bio had the right idea, but the story is bigger than "sensual" selections: Monotropa fits a Seattle lineage of patient, human-scale dance floors. In Shameless terms, expect warmth, slow-burn tension, and a room that gets pulled in rather than pushed around.
Seattle-based DJ active in the local electronic music community.

Nancy Dru is a Vancouver, BC-based techno DJ and producer with more than 20 years in electronic music. Her path started with online radio in the 1990s, moved through IDM, jungle, and ambient production, and evolved into dense, danceable techno marked by syncopated beats and acid bite. She has played alongside artists including Steffi, Carl Craig, and Rødhåd, with festival appearances tied to Mutek, Decibel, and Communikey. Nancy Dru brings Pacific Northwest techno with real architecture: brainy, bodily, and built from long-term commitment.
Portland, OR DJ duo (Michael McKinnon and Matthew Quiet). Host the Limited Edition parties and I Believe It's Magic radio show.

Nick Carroll is a Seattle DJ, booker, and co-founder of secondnature. Through secondnature, Research, and Kremwerk-connected work, he helped carve out space for sharper techno, house, bass pressure, and late-night programming in the Pacific Northwest. As a DJ, he can move from heavy techno into house, classic dubstep, and disco without making it feel like a resume exercise. Nick brings range, taste, and a little danger to the floor.

Nina Sol is an Oakland-based DJ with a deep, soulful touch and a collector's ear for music that makes people feel held while they move. Her sets draw from soul, funk, house, disco, and diasporic groove, with residencies and Bay Area parties built around community as much as selection. She is not a rush-the-room kind of DJ; she lets warmth do the work until the floor is fully open. Nina brings sunlight, rhythm, and that rare kind of dance-floor generosity.

Noise Complaint is a Seattle DJ and events collective built around thoughtfully curated house and techno nights. With long ties to the Kremwerk/Timbre Room ecosystem, the project has helped create space for touring guests, local selectors, and dance floors that want more intention than bottle-service wallpaper. The name is cheeky, but the work is serious: building rooms where underground music can feel social, sweaty, and specific. In the Shameless universe, Noise Complaint fits naturally,part promoter brain, part resident heat, and fully committed to keeping Seattle loud in the right ways.

Nordic Soul is Sean Horton, the DJ, producer, composer, and Decibel Festival founder whose fingerprints are all over Seattle electronic music. Under the Nordic Soul name, he channels the same wide-angle taste that made Decibel matter: techno, house, hip-hop, soul, live instrumentation, and emotionally charged electronic music without genre tunnel vision. His work behind the scenes helped connect Seattle to global experimental and club culture, while his DJ sets keep that history physical and floor-focused. In the Shameless world, Nordic Soul is both elder statesman and active voltage,deep roots, deep crates, and a room-first sense of momentum.

Project 32 is Steven, a Seattle electronic artist whose public profile keeps the description simple: synths and things. Listings place Project 32 in the city's live electronic circuit, including Kremwerk support for LA techno figure Truncate and Shameless' Memorial Day Hijinks at Monkey Loft. The project leans hardware-minded and direct, with compact live electronic energy built for the room.

Raica is the ambient and experimental electronic project of Seattle artist Chloe Harris, a veteran DJ, label operator, and lifelong record obsessive. Harris started in record stores young, began DJing as a teenager, and went on to help run Further Records while developing Raica as a space for improvisational, textural electronics. The sound drifts between ambient, techno, and hardware-driven abstraction, built for deep listening but never disconnected from the body. Raica brings a different kind of intensity: fog, circuitry, pressure, and the kind of patience that makes a room lean in.
Seattle electronic artist and DJ. Known for hardware-based live sets and a whimsical approach to music production.

Repoman is a Seattle DJ, promoter, and graphic designer whose sets pull from EBM, electro, wave, acid, synth, and darker club strains. Club YES lineups with Shameless, False Prophet, and Night Moves place Repoman in the city's circuit for past-present-future body music. The sound is sharp, stylish, and a little deviant, built for dancers who like the room dim and the drums metallic.

Robag Wruhme is Gabor Schablitzki, the German producer and DJ whose catalog has become a benchmark for playful, detailed, emotionally strange house and techno. Known through Kompakt, Pampa, Musik Krause, and the Wighnomy Brothers lineage, he makes dance music that can be microscopic and goofy, elegant and crooked, all at once. His productions reward close listening, but they also understand that the club is not a lecture hall. Robag brings master-level weirdness,melody, mischief, and precision folded into one unmistakable bounce.

Rosie is a Seattle yoga instructor connected to Reverie Society's Shameless yoga sessions. Her role sits in the softer edge of the roster: movement, music, breath, and the daytime community side of the crew's programming.

Shaner is a Seattle DJ tied to the modern Shameless Breakfast Club circle, with public listings placing him on the 2026 New Year's Day lineup at Monkey Loft and a SoundCloud profile for the SHANERISM series. His footprint is local, social, and built around the long-haul Breakfast Club format. Shaner brings lived-in Seattle energy, steady dance-floor instincts, and a sound that fits naturally beside Recess, Riz, WD4D, Sho-Nuph, Veta Vitali, and the rest of the crew.

Simpa is a Seattle DJ with roots reaching back to 1993 and a deep connection to house and drum & bass culture. Public profiles frame Simpa as an electronic music veteran with a sharp ear for rhythm, bassline movement, and immersive energy. That long arc matters in a city where dance music history is carried by people who kept showing up before it was convenient. In a Shameless room, Simpa brings experience, pace, and the steady hand of someone who knows exactly where the floor can go.

Sinca is a Montréal-based DJ and producer whose melodic house and techno carry the warmth of her French-Canadian and Peruvian roots. Her releases on labels including Purified, Bedrock, and Anjunadeep have put her in the international conversation without flattening the personality out of her sound. She builds sets with lift and precision, balancing lush melodic pressure with drums that still know how to work a dark room. Sinca brings sunrise energy with backbone,polished, emotional, and built for dancers who want both beauty and drive.

SUBSET is Karl Muller, a New Zealand-born, Australia-based DJ and producer working in the deep end of minimal dub techno and dubtronica. His sound lives in reverbs, filtered delays, and slow-pressure architecture, with releases connected to labels such as Rohs\! Records, Telrae, DimbiDeep, and Primary Colours. Rather than chase obvious peaks, SUBSET builds immersion through bass weight, atmosphere, and details that keep expanding the longer you stay with them. The result is a heady, low-lit trip for dancers who want hypnosis with sub pressure and no cheap shortcuts.

Sunshine Jones is a San Francisco house lifer, founding member of Dubtribe Sound System, and one of the rare electronic performers who can make machines feel handmade in real time. Since the late 1980s, he has carried a fiercely DIY philosophy through live improvisational sets, solo work, and the global Dubtribe legacy. His music is rooted in love, community, and radical participation, but it still hits with the physical truth of proper house music. Sunshine Jones is not nostalgia,he is living proof that underground dance music can be spiritual, political, and wildly functional all at once.

T.Wan is Tiffany Wan, a Brooklyn-based DJ with deep Seattle origins and a long-running connection to the femme, non-binary, and trans collective TUF. Her monthly Flow State show on Noods Radio reflects the range: techno, jungle, breaks, garage, dubstep, footwork, acid, and left-field club music all moving toward immersion. Before New York rooms like Nowadays, Basement, and Good Room, she cut her teeth in Seattle as a DJ, organizer, and tone-setter for adventurous dance floors. T.Wan brings cerebral bass pressure with real body logic,trippy, percussive, and never stuck in one lane.
Pacific Northwest DJ blazing a trail for West Coast dance music since 2002. Known for a sensuous, genre-defying sound.

A glittering journey into the magical world of showtunes and musical theatre. From the early Shameless Breakfast Club era, preserved on the 2014 New Year's Day lineup at Monkey Loft. Billed alongside Area 303, Gato Negro, Dirty Ice, Adlib, Joe Bellingham, Sone, Chad Neiro, Ramiro, Recess, Julian O'Keefe, Sean Majors, Verse, Carlos R, Hydro Craft, and Miss Shelrawka, the project sits squarely in the crew's glitter-streaked afterhours history.

Totally Enormous Extinct Dinosaurs is Orlando Higginbottom, the British producer, DJ, and singer-songwriter behind some of the most distinctive left-field pop and house of the last decade-plus. From the colorful early TEED era through later work on Anjunadeep and beyond, his music has always balanced club architecture with melody, melancholy, and oddball charm. He writes dance music that can live in headphones, festival fields, and basement rooms without losing its strange little heartbeat. In a Shameless setting, TEED brings rare crossover magic: emotional, playful, deeply produced, and still built to move bodies.

Unknown Player, also listed in some event indexes as Unknown ?layer, is a Seattle DJ tied to Habitat, The Reason Why, Uniting Souls, Viva Recordings, and Shameless day-party programming. Listings place the name on Reverie Society at Monkey Loft with Black Velveteen, Mz ArTiz, and Blk Lab, plus other Seattle events at Monkey Loft and Kremwerk. The project lives through local lineups and house-centered community rooms, with an underground profile that fits the name.

Viper Fengz is a Vietnamese-born Seattle drag artist and club DJ known for high-energy queer nightlife. Recent RA listings call her a Seattle drag legend and place her alongside parties leaning into hyperpop, Jersey club, baile funk, techno remixes, and full internet-brain club chaos. That tracks with the performance-first side of her work: loud, fast, funny, and committed to the bit without losing the floor. Viper Fengz brings drag electricity and rave disorder in equal measure.

wet peynt is a Seattle live electronic artist and producer working in a playful, machine-driven lane of IDM, tech house, beats, and hardware experimentation. Public artist pages point to Octatrack, Digitakt, SE-02, Peak, Play, M8, and modular gear as part of the setup, with finished releases on Bandcamp and streaming platforms plus demo uploads on SoundCloud. Event history places wet peynt in the Shameless/Desert Hearts orbit for Memorial Day Hijinks at Monkey Loft and in Seattle's live-electronic ecosystem through Patchwerks and Basement State. wet peynt brings a left-field live PA feel: odd textures, skippy grooves, and dance music that keeps its handmade edges visible.

Wonder Twinz is the Seattle duo of Ms. Artiz and Julie Herrera, a pairing that shows up in regional festival and Duck Brunch/Shameless-adjacent listings. The project connects two established local community selectors into a playful back-to-back format, with public lineups placing them near names like Levi Clark, Cat Call, Claudia Martinez, Sappho, and other PNW underground artists. Wonder Twinz brings bright communal energy: two DJs trading taste, movement, and room-reading while keeping the floor friendly and alive.

ZOF is Zoe, a Seattle-based producer, vocalist, DJ, and classically trained pianist turning personal reclamation into exploratory club music. After leaving a demanding science career in the Pacific Northwest, she pushed fully into music, landing releases and support through HE.SHE.THEY., Popgang, Box of Cats, TECHNE, and Dome of Doom. Her sound moves through bass, house, breaks, and left-field electronics with a songwriter's ear for emotional hooks and a producer's hunger for strange texture. ZOF brings a future-facing Seattle energy: smart, raw, melodic, and unwilling to sound like anybody else on the flyer.

Abraham is a Seattle DJ documented on the Studio 4/4 stage at Shameless' 7th Memorial Day Hijinks at Monkey Loft. That 2022 lineup placed him alongside Black Velveteen, Jesse Leer, Simone BG, and Snapdragon, with Desert Hearts on the rooftop and Shameless residents in the loft. Abraham lands as local support from the house and techno side of the Seattle circuit, connected to Studio 4/4 and made for daytime rooftop momentum.

Acid Tourist is Holmar, formerly of Thugfucker, bringing a seasoned, psychedelic approach to house and techno. His work under the Acid Tourist name keeps the focus on movement, atmosphere, and the loose edges where club music gets strange. Seattle lineups have placed him in rooms connected to long-running house culture, including Flammable and Uniting Souls circles. Acid Tourist brings road-worn, trippy club energy built for dancers who stay late.

Seattle-based DJ and co-founder of OFF99 alongside Keenan Stevenson. Their West Coast house project draws from soul, disco, jazz, and hip-hop influences. OFF99 has hosted legends including Omar S, Fred P, DJ QU, and Palms Trax, and launched the HouseOFF99 mix series spotlighting Pacific Northwest DJs. A regular presence at Kremwerk and Monkey Loft.

PNW DJ and promoter active since 2010. A steady presence in Seattle's underground dance music scene, Alex advocates for techno, industrial, and darker 4/4 sounds. Co-founder and promoter with Depth & Macabre events in Seattle. Plays regularly at Kremwerk and other Seattle underground venues.

Alex the Cosmic Therapist is a Seattle DJ/live artist appearing in Shameless-adjacent community listings, including Breakfast Club with Wajatta, DJ Heather, The Fungineers, B.Fly, Black Velveteen, Riz, Recess, Tek Jones, Shannon O, WD4D, and other Northwest fixtures. Additional RA listings place Alex in the Grooving for Good resident circle, where the programming leans hip-hop, pop, funk, mid-tempo, downtempo, alternative, and electronic music. Alex the Cosmic Therapist fits as an eclectic connector: playful, healing-room energy with enough rhythm to land in a club setting, and a name that signals mood as much as genre.

Seattle-native DJ with a deep love for minimal beats and the underground electronic music scene. Part of the Between the Lines City Collective and a regular performer in both Seattle and Los Angeles. Known for drawing out the emotion in minimal and underground sounds.

Seattle-based DJ immersed in the electronic music scene since the late 1990s. A thoughtful selector with a wealth of experience in underground dance music. Regular performer at Kremwerk, Monkey Loft, and other Pacific Northwest venues, often playing b2b with Audie Dimitri.
Seattle-based DJ and live performer bringing dynamic sets to the underground dance scene. A regular presence in the city's electronic music community with an energetic, crowd-focused approach.

Ana-Lucid is Ana-Lisa, a Pacific Northwest producer and DJ shaping minimal beats, ethereal soundscapes, driving basslines, and deep grooves. Her public profiles connect her to Restless Planet, Makes Me Human, Flammable, Space Yacht, Between The Lines, Monkey Loft, Substation, and Kremwerk orbit. Since beginning to curate her sound in 2020, she has moved toward groovy, whimsical, spiritually tuned club music with a real local footprint.

Anabel Englund is a Southern California singer, songwriter, producer, and DJ whose voice helped define a polished, emotionally direct corner of modern house music. Her collaborations with Hot Natured, MK, Lee Foss, Jamie Jones, and Hot Creations turned into major club moments, while later work on AREA10, Ultra, Defected, and her debut album Messing with Magic pushed her further into dance-pop territory. She carries hooks like a pop writer but understands the physical payoff of a house record. Anabel brings crossover glamour with club credibility,big vocals, real groove, and no shortage of lift.

Anakim is Riccardo LeBron, a Los Angeles-based DJ and producer whose own phrase for the sound is "Intergalactic Underground Sounds." His melodic house and techno leans cinematic, futuristic, dark, and emotionally charged, with concept-driven releases and a style shaped by Icon Collective, LA club culture, and big-room underground stages. The music is polished but not soft, often building suspense before opening into something huge. Anakim brings sci-fi drama, melodic pressure, and a little cosmic menace.

Vancouver-based DJ and producer originally from El Salvador, known for blending lush melodies with driving percussion for an emotive dance floor experience. Owner of Rainforest Music label. An active presence in the Pacific Northwest underground electronic music circuit.

Seattle-based DJ known for a nostalgic, daytime-dancing flavor blending house and classic sounds. A regular at Monkey Loft and part of the Restless Planet crew, bringing warm, soul-driven sets to the Pacific Northwest underground scene.

Seattle-based DJ, co-host of the Jola Madi Podcast, and organizer behind Curated Vibez and Free Agent Seattle. An active community member in Seattle's dance music scene, bringing diverse sounds and strong connections to events like Blast Music Fest.

Aos is a Seattle producer, DJ, organizer, and member of secondnature and TUF. Her background runs through Tacoma DIY spaces, KUPS college radio, queer community work, and the textured edge of the Northwest underground. The sound is slippery and emotional: rhythmic enough for the floor, strange enough to keep the room awake. In Shameless language, Aos is sunrise music with teeth.

Area 303 is a Seattle live techno and acid project associated with Larry Kleinke and Wilf Libgott, built around hardware-driven performance rather than laptop polish. Press around their live setup points to drum machines, acid lines, and improvisation. Area 303 brings the classic machine-room feeling: raw sequences, squelch, and Seattle techno with its sleeves rolled up.

Aria Bare is a queer techno producer and DJ rooted in the Pacific Northwest underground. Their music favors atmosphere, hardware-minded texture, and the kind of shadowy momentum that works as well in a DIY room as it does in a late-night club slot. As a selector, Aria Bare keeps the focus on tension and release rather than obvious shortcuts, letting experimental instincts sit inside functional techno pressure. The set comes with teeth and atmosphere: intimate, physical, and tuned for dancers who like the edges left sharp.

Ashley Younniä is a Los Angeles-based DJ, curator, and performing artist with a sound rooted in Black, queer, and femme-led house culture. Her work with parties like Stardust and TheyHouseLA has helped shape a soulful, sweaty corner of LA's underground. She plays with groove first, keeping the room lifted without sanding down the edges. A Shameless set from Ashley lands in that sweet spot between deep feeling, body music, and full dance-floor release.

Seattle DJ (Atlee Treasure) who has been active in the city's nightclub and radio scene since the early 2000s. Co-founded the Trust party series in 2009 alongside Kid Hops and Jason Justice. Owner of the Momentos Records label and music project Rozalesdmusic.

Audie Dimitri is a Seattle-based DJ known for all-vinyl dance parties, deep house, and underground club sounds. She has appeared on Innerflight Broadcast and plays local rooms including The Rendezvous and Monkey Loft. She often plays back-to-back with Alice Camille at Shameless and Reverie Society events.

Seattle-based DJ bringing soulful and rhythmic sounds to the underground dance scene. A presence at Monkey Loft and other Seattle venues, playing alongside the city's finest DJs for 1luv events and beyond.

Seattle-based DJ delivering energetic, genre-crossing sets in the underground electronic music scene. A regular presence at local events, bringing powerful dance floor energy and a dynamic range of electronic sounds.

Seattle-based DJ, producer, and owner of AVIGNON ENTERTAINMENT (real name Michael Robertson). Influenced by DJ SNEAK, Low Steppa, OFFAIAH, and Franky Rizardo. A featured guest on C89.5 Seattle Summer Sessions and a regular presence at Seattle underground events and Shameless parties.

B.LakePeterson is a Seattle DJ active across community-driven dance spaces, from Belltown Art Walk sets to Monkey Loft lineups and Shameless-linked events. Listings connect the name to Reverie Society's Cascadia Winter Solstice closing party, Deck'd Out season-closure programming, and Breakfast Club. The sound lands in a social, room-aware lane built for art spaces, daytime dance floors, and late-night house and techno gatherings.

Seattle-based DJ (Casey Berner) delivering tech house and deep house to the city's queer underground and club scene. A regular at The Cuff Complex, Monkey Loft, and Reverie Society events. Plays everything from deep house to EDM and melodic vibes.
Seattle DJ known for sultry deep house and dirty, high-energy heaters. A fixture in Seattle's local lady DJ scene.

MMBASSY is a Pacific Northwest-based all-women DJ collective and events platform built to elevate women across electronic music. Their mission is bigger than a lineup gimmick: they produce events, panels, workshops, and dance-floor experiences that put women in command as artists, producers, organizers, and culture-makers. Projects like See Her, Be Her turn representation into action, connecting skill-building with actual rooms full of music. MMBASSY brings infrastructure and fire,community work you can dance to.

Seattle-based EDM music events collective and DJ crew representing the Pacific Northwest underground scene. Milo House Presents hosts multi-stage events at venues like Vue Seattle and Q Nightclub, spotlighting local and emerging artists in house, dubstep, and electronic music.
Seattle-based DJ bringing organic, natural-sounding electronic music to the underground dance scene. Known for lush, layered sounds and an immersive approach to dance floor storytelling.

West Coast festival DJ with deep house to techno roots going back to 1995. A top West Coast festival performer with credits at Lightning in a Bottle, Desert Hearts, Shambhala, Oregon Eclipse, and Photosynthesis. Runs B2P booking agency, placing acts across the West Coast from San Diego to Seattle.

Seattle-based DJ camp and event collective founded in 2016. Known for throwing booty-forward, body-positive dance parties at venues like Nectar Lounge. Affiliated with DJ Cheeks and Neon Lounge NW. A fun and inclusive presence in the Seattle underground dance scene.

Chicago-born DJ now based in Seattle as a resident. A regular at Shameless Productions' Deck'd Out summer series and vinyl sets at Monkey Loft. Known for a diverse, free-flowing style that spans house, live-streamed sets, and intimate club performances across Seattle.

Seattle-based Nepalese-American DJ and co-founder of Caravan Seattle. One half of the Silk Road DJ duo alongside Yelda (an Afghan-American DJ), sharing global beats that span world music, electronic, and beyond. A community builder in Seattle's diverse dance music scene.
New Jersey-born, Seattle-based DJ (John Labrador). Known for progressive house with Latin and tech house influences.

Blueyedsoul, also known as Big Room Benny or Benny Bitters, is a Seattle-based DJ bringing house music and big-room lift to the Pacific Northwest. A Capitol Hill Block Party regular with Neumos and Barboza ties, he plays for rooms that want familiar energy, local confidence, and a clean party read.
Atlanta-based DJ and producer. Creative Director at Satya Records. Co-founder of Zemya Fest.

Brit Hansen is a Seattle electronic music fixture whose work stretches from the beloved tech-house duo Jacob London to the left-field solo world of Hanssen. KEXP describes her as an integral figure in the local electronic community for more than two decades, and that history shows in the way she moves between weirdness and dance-floor function. Her solo direction leans cinematic, dreamy, and synth-heavy, while her DJ instincts still know exactly how to pull a room into hypnotic motion. Brit brings the rare combination of scene memory and restless curiosity,deep roots, strange colors, and zero autopilot.

Brooke Would is a Seattle selector connected to the funky side of underground house. Her SoundCloud trail under The Funky Kind points toward sets that favor groove, warmth, and the kind of low-key swing that makes a room loosen up without being pushed. The name reads playful, but the lane is practical: records that keep bodies moving and leave room for personality. Brooke Would brings the fun without flattening the taste.
